stats.fact: 85% of developers now use at least one AI tool in their workflow, with 51% using AI tools daily
Organizations moving from 0% to 100% AI adoption saw median cycle time fall from 16.7 to 12.7 hours (24% improvement), while developer output rose 76%
AI coding tools reduce time on routine coding tasks by 46%, according to McKinsey survey of 4,500 developers across 150 enterprises
~3.6 hours saved per developer per week on average using AI coding tools
73% of engineering teams now use AI coding tools daily, up from 41% in 2025
GitHub Copilot developers complete JavaScript HTTP server tasks 55% faster; pull request time dropped from 9.6 days to 2.4 days (75% reduction)
AI coding tools generate 41% of all code worldwide as of 2026, with developers reporting 42% of their code is AI-generated or assisted
stats.source: Second Talent AI Coding Assistant Statistics 2026

stats.fact: Only 29% of developers trust AI coding output despite 84% using or planning to use AI tools; 61% say AI often produces code that looks correct but is not reliable
Projects using too much AI-generated code experienced a 41% rise in bugs and a 7.2% drop in system stability
AI-coauthored PRs generate 1.7x more review issues than human-only PRs, causing a 91% spike in review volume
stats.source: Faros AI Productivity Paradox Research Report

stats.fact: Healthy ROI on AI coding tools: 2.5-3.5x average, 4-6x top quartile; enterprise average $37.50 cost per incremental PR vs $150 developer time saved (4:1 return)
stats.source: blog.exceeds.ai Enterprise AI Coding ROI Studies 2026

stats.fact: Most enterprises report positive ROI within 3 to 6 months of adopting AI coding tools
Teams with strong context files report 40-60% less AI-generated code needing rewriting
stats.source: Fordel Studios AI Coding Assistants Cost Per Engineer 2026

stats.fact: GitHub Copilot suggestion acceptance rate: 35-40% as of 2026, with 90% of developers committing code suggested by Copilot
Controlled studies found developers took 19% longer to complete tasks with AI tools than without, despite expecting a 24% speedup and believing they were 20% faster
stats.source: Longitudinal case study published on arXiv 2025
